name: Rotisserie Weingrün
note: Firuze wollte da mal mit mir hingehen. Good candidate for a nicer dinner in Mitte.
description: Stylish Berlin-Mitte rotisserie restaurant built around an open flame grill. Known for rotisserie and grill dishes like landhuhn, flammlachs, Barbarie duck, entrecôte, and Duroc pork belly, with a more polished date-night or guest-dinner feel than a casual neighborhood spot.
what_to_order: Landhuhn or flammlachs from the rotisserie, plus a couple of sides and wine

name: Restaurant Tsomi — Georgian Kitchen
note: Georgian restaurant in Kollwitzkiez. Good candidate for a shared dinner spot or dinner with guests.
description: Modern Georgian restaurant in Prenzlauer Berg’s Kollwitzkiez that leans into warm hospitality and classic supra-style table sharing. The kitchen mixes traditional Georgian dishes with Persian, Ottoman, and Anatolian influences, and the menu explicitly calls out strong vegetarian and vegan options.
what_to_order: Tsomi appetizer platter to share, plus a few Georgian mains and wine

name: Pala Berlin — Greek Café
note: Found by my wife, need to try for brunch.
description: Greek family café from Rhodes, now in Prenzlauer Berg. Ingredients imported directly from Greece — feta, olive oil, honey, yoghurt. Known for bougatsa (custard pastry), Greek orange cake, and iced coffees from Blaue Bohne roastery. Leafy hidden gem with indoor and outdoor seating. No laptops allowed. Weekends get busy — go early.
what_to_order: Bougatsa or Melitzana, Greek orange cake + iced coffee

name: Bake and Take
note: Recommended by my wife after our disastrous cake hunt. Need to check it out for pastries and cake.
description: Small family bakery in Friedrichshain’s Samariterviertel, opened in 2024 and already a neighborhood favorite. Known for laminated pastries made by hand with French butter and Italian flour, plus excellent coffee to go.
what_to_order: Classic croissant, cardamom bun, salted caramel croissant, or a cruffin
